# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Http;
use Symfony\Component\DomCrawler\Crawler;

class LinkPreviewController extends Controller
{
    public function generatePreview(Request $request)
    {
        $url = $request->input('url');
        
        if (!$url) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'URL is required'], 400);
        }
        
        try {
            $response = Http::timeout(10)->get($url);
            
            if ($response->successful()) {
                $html = $response->body();
                $crawler = new Crawler($html);
                
                $title = $crawler->filter('title')->first()->text() ?? '';
                $description = $crawler->filter('meta[name="description"]')->first()->attr('content') ?? '';
                $image = $crawler->filter('meta[property="og:image"]')->first()->attr('content') ?? '';
                
                if (!$image) {
                    $image = $crawler->filter('meta[name="twitter:image"]')->first()->attr('content') ?? '';
                }
                
                if (!$image) {
                    $image = $crawler->filter('img')->first()->attr('src') ?? '';
                }
                
                $favicon = $this->getFavicon($url);
                
                return response()->json([
                    'url' => $url,
                    'title' => $title,
                    'description' => $description,
                    'image' => $image,
                    'favicon' => $favicon,
                ]);
            } else {
                return response()->json(['error' => 'Failed to fetch URL'], 400);
            }
        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Failed to fetch URL'], 400);
        }
    }
    
    private function getFavicon($url)
    {
        $parsedUrl = parse_url($url);
        $faviconUrl = $parsedUrl['scheme'] . '://' . $parsedUrl['host'] . '/favicon.ico';
        
        try {
            $response = Http::timeout(5)->get($faviconUrl);
            if ($response->successful()) {
                return $faviconUrl;
            }
        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            return null;
        }
        
        return null;
    }
}
